title: Washington walls off Anthropic, new trillionaire Musk says money won't matter - TCR 06/13/26
description:
The Commerce Department forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide days after launch, SpaceX closed its first trading day above $2 trillion to make its owner the world's first trillionaire, and Data Center Watch logged $130 billion in projects blocked or delayed across 75 sites in three months.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-13-2026/

0:00 Washington forces Anthropic to disable its best models
7:13 SpaceX lists and the first trillionaire
12:01 The $130 billion data center revolt
16:29 AI agents gain live execution paths
19:22 A laser phase plate reveals the hidden proteome
22:31 Close

This episode covers the Anthropic export-control shutdown alongside the lapse of Section 702 surveillance authority, SpaceX's record listing and the trillionaire's claim that money will stop mattering, the $130 billion data center revolt and the unsupported China-influence narrative, AI agents gaining live execution paths into Coinbase, Visa, and enterprise code, and a laser phase plate bringing the hidden proteome into view.

A Commerce Department directive placed Anthropic's most capable models under export controls over a reported jailbreak the company says involved only minor flaws other public models already find, forcing a worldwide disablement that split access by nationality. The same week, SpaceX opened above $150 a share and closed near a $2.1 trillion valuation as its owner predicted AI and robotic labor would make currency irrelevant, even as he holds record wealth in the rockets, compute, and factories that would still carry power without it. Data Center Watch counted 75 projects worth $130 billion blocked or delayed as opposition groups doubled to 833 across 49 states, while OpenAI and others tied the backlash to China on evidence researchers say does not hold up. AI agents crossed from advice into action across Coinbase trading, Visa checkout inside ChatGPT, and Codex running in enterprise clouds. And a laser phase plate built into a cryo-electron microscope could lift native-cell protein visibility from under 1% toward more than half.

- WIRED: The Data Center Backlash Is Being Blamed on China: https://www.wired.com/story/china-us-data-center-opposition/
